landing.statsDesc: Live statistics showing how Meyaar helps you achieve your financial goals

landing.footerTagline: Designed carefully for smarter financial management

faq.a1: Meyaar is a personal budgeting app that helps you track your expenses, set financial goals, and calculate zakat — everything in one place with a smooth design.
faq.a2: Yes, Meyaar is completely free. You can create an account and start immediately without any fees or subscriptions.
faq.a3: Meyaar relies on calculating the Islamic nisab based on updated gold prices, and automatically calculates the zakat due from your savings at 2.5%.
faq.a4: Your data is fully encrypted during transmission and storage. We use the latest security technologies to protect your financial privacy.
faq.q5: Can I use it on mobile?
faq.a5: Absolutely! Meyaar is designed to work seamlessly on all devices — mobile, tablet, and laptop — with a fully responsive experience.

zakatPage.title: Zakat Calculator
zakatPage.subtitle: Calculate your zakat easily according to Sharia
zakatPage.banner: ✨ Your zakat is a blessing in your wealth ✨
zakatPage.infoText: 💡 Zakat is a pillar of Islam. It is obligatory on every Muslim whose wealth reaches the nisab ({{grams}} grams of gold ≈ {{value}}) and a complete lunar year has passed.
zakatPage.shariahText: Zakat is obligatory on wealth when it reaches the nisab (equivalent to {{grams}} grams of gold = {{value}}) and a complete Islamic year passes. Zakat rate is 2.5% of total wealth.
zakatPage.source: Source: Kuwait Encyclopedia of Islamic Jurisprudence, Zakat Section
zakatPage.reminderSet: You'll be reminded of the next zakat date ⭐
zakatPage.amountPositive: Amount must be greater than zero

education.title: Financial Education
education.subtitle: Learn money management basics
education.lesson_budgeting_desc: Learn how to organize your monthly budget effectively
education.bb_step1: A budget is a financial plan that defines your income and expenses for a specific period. The golden rule is 50/30/20: 50% for needs, 30% for wants, 20% for savings.
education.bb_step2: 💡 Start by tracking your expenses for a full month before setting a budget. You'll discover where your money actually goes!
education.bb_step3: What is the golden rule for budgeting?
education.lesson_saving_desc: Discover smart ways to save money
education.ss_step1: Saving isn't what's left after spending — it's the first thing you do when you receive your salary. Pay yourself first!
education.ss_step2: 💡 Use the envelope method: allocate an envelope for each spending category. When the money in the envelope runs out, stop spending in that category.
education.ss_q1_a: Pay yourself first when you receive your salary
education.ss_q1_b: Save whatever is left at the end of the month
education.lesson_invest_desc: Learn the basics and types of investing
education.ii_step1: Investing is putting your money into assets that grow in value over time. Main types: stocks, bonds, real estate, and investment funds.
education.ii_step2: Diversification is the key to successful investing. Don't put all your eggs in one basket. Spread your investments across different asset types.
education.ii_step3: 💡 Start investing early even with small amounts. The power of compound interest increases over time!
education.ii_step4: What is the most important principle in investing?
education.ii_q1_a: Put all money in one stock
education.lesson_zakat_desc: Learn the Islamic guidelines for zakat
education.zr_step1: Zakat is one of the five pillars of Islam. It's obligatory on every adult Muslim who owns wealth equal to the nisab (85 grams of gold) for a full lunar year.
education.zr_step2: 💡 The zakat rate is 2.5% of total wealth that has reached the nisab and a full lunar year has passed.
education.zr_step3: What is the prescribed zakat rate?
education.lesson_debt_desc: Learn how to handle debt wisely
education.dm_step1: There are two common methods for paying off debt: the Snowball method (start with the smallest) and the Avalanche method (start with highest interest). The first gives you psychological motivation.
education.dm_step2: 💡 Always try to pay more than the minimum required amount. Even a small extra amount significantly reduces interest.
education.dm_step3: What is the Snowball method for paying off debt?
education.dm_q1_a: Pay off the smallest debt first
education.dm_q1_b: Pay off the largest debt first
education.lesson_emergency_desc: Why you need an emergency fund and how to build one
education.ef_step1: An emergency fund is a saved amount that covers your expenses for 3-6 months in case of job loss or medical emergencies.
education.ef_step2: 💡 Start with a small goal (one month of expenses) then gradually increase it. Keep the amount in a separate account from your main account.
education.ef_step3: How much should an emergency fund cover?
education.lesson_compound_desc: Discover how compound interest works in your favor
education.ci_step1: Compound interest is interest on interest. If you invest 1000 OMR at 10% annually, after one year you'll have 1100, and after two years 1210 (not 1200).
education.ci_step2: Rule of 72: Divide 72 by the return rate to know how many years needed to double your money. Example: 72 ÷ 8 = 9 years.
education.ci_step3: 💡 The earlier you start, the greater the effect of compound interest. Time is the most important factor!
education.ci_step4: According to the Rule of 72, how many years to double money at 12% return?
education.lesson_islamic_desc: Principles of Sharia-compliant financing and investing
education.if_step1: Islamic finance is based on principles: prohibition of riba (interest), prohibition of gharar (excessive risk), and profit-loss sharing.
education.if_step2: Islamic financing instruments include: Murabaha, Mudaraba, Musharaka, Ijara, and Sukuk. All are Sharia-compliant alternatives to interest-based loans.
education.if_step3: 💡 When investing in stocks, ensure the company doesn't deal in riba and its activities are halal.
education.if_step4: Which of the following is a fundamental principle in Islamic finance?
education.if_q1_a: Prohibition of riba and profit-loss sharing

tips.reduceExpenses: Your expenses are over 70% of income. Try reducing non-essential spending first.
tips.startSmall: As a beginner, start with small amounts and increase gradually as you learn.
tips.diversifyStocks: High stock allocation — make sure to diversify across different sectors.
tips.sukukSafe: Sukuk provide stable returns with lower risk — great for your profile.
tips.lowRiskOk: Conservative investing is smart — it protects your capital while providing steady growth.
tips.bigGoalPlan: For major goals like a house or marriage, consider a structured saving plan alongside investments.
tips.consistentSaving: Consistency is key — invest the same amount every month regardless of market conditions.
